rootCauseAnalysis.summary: ClusterIssuer 'demo-selfsigned-ca' is not Ready because its backing CA secret 'demo-ca-key-pair' does not exist, blocking TLS certificate issuance for the demo-app deployment. The missing secret prevents the ClusterIssuer from signing CertificateRequests, leaving the TLS secret 'demo-app-tls' absent and pods serving without valid HTTPS.
contributingFactors: ClusterIssuer 'demo-selfsigned-ca' reports: 'Error getting keypair for CA issuer: secrets "demo-ca-key-pair" not found'
The CA secret 'demo-ca-key-pair' is absent from the cluster — it was never created or was deleted
CertificateRequest 'demo-app-cert-2' is stuck in Pending with message: 'Referenced issuer does not have a Ready status condition'
The TLS secret 'demo-app-tls' does not exist in the namespace — cert-manager cannot issue it because the issuer is broken
Certificate 'demo-app-cert' transitioned from Ready=True to Ready=False (DoesNotExist) after the secret was deleted/expired
The Deployment mounts 'demo-app-tls' with optional:true, so pods start but serve without valid TLS certificates

kaResponse.analysis: ClusterIssuer 'demo-selfsigned-ca' is not Ready because its backing CA secret 'demo-ca-key-pair' does not exist, blocking TLS certificate issuance for the demo-app deployment. The missing secret prevents the ClusterIssuer from signing CertificateRequests, leaving the TLS secret 'demo-app-tls' absent and pods serving without valid HTTPS.
